title: Buttondown vs Sendinblue (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Buttondown vs Sendinblue. See pricing, features, and which email marketing tool is best for your needs.
intro: Buttondown and Sendinblue (now Brevo) serve different email marketing needs with distinct strengths. Buttondown caters to developers and writers who prioritize simplicity and markdown-based workflows, while Sendinblue offers a comprehensive CRM platform with SMS, transactional email, and advanced automation. Your choice depends on whether you need a focused newsletter tool or an all-in-one business communication suite.

comparison_table.tool_a: Markdown writing, API access, paid subscriptions
comparison_table.tool_b: SMS, WhatsApp, chat, CRM, 300 free emails/day

choose_a_if: You're a technical writer or developer who loves markdown and wants a simple, focused newsletter tool
You want the lowest entry price and prefer API-first integrations
You plan to offer paid subscriptions to your readers and need built-in support for that model
choose_b_if: You need an all-in-one platform combining email, SMS, WhatsApp, and CRM capabilities
You want a much larger free tier with 100,000 subscribers and 300 daily emails included
You run an ecommerce business or send transactional emails and need advanced automations

faq.a: Neither is objectively better—they serve different purposes. Buttondown excels for writers and developers seeking simplicity and markdown support, while Sendinblue offers comprehensive business communication features including SMS, CRM, and chat. Your choice depends on whether you need a focused newsletter tool or a broader platform.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Buttondown or Sendinblue?
faq.a: For small operations, Sendinblue is cheaper thanks to its generous free tier supporting 100,000 subscribers and 300 daily emails at no cost. Buttondown's free tier caps at 100 subscribers. However, Buttondown's paid tier starts at $9/month versus Sendinblue's $25/month, making it more economical if you need paid features with fewer subscribers.

faq.q: Can I switch from Sendinblue to Buttondown?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support import/export functionality. Buttondown includes explicit import/export features, allowing you to migrate your subscriber lists and content. The process is straightforward, though you may lose some platform-specific features like SMS campaigns during the switch, as they don't exist in Buttondown.
